topic: A person accidentally sends a text to the wrong recipient
raw_output:
[story 1]

My thumb hovered over the screen, a nervous tremor running through my hand. I was trying to send a quick, innocuous message to Sarah about meeting up, but my finger slipped. Instead of Sarah’s contact, the message zipped out to a number I didn't recognize. A cold knot tightened in my stomach as I watched the 'sent' notification flash. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. Every muscle in my body seemed to lock up. I stared at the phone, the screen glowing accusingly, waiting for some terrible consequence to materialize. A sudden, sharp intake of air was the only sound in the quiet room.

[story 2]

The notification popped up, bright and undeniable, and I felt the blood drain from my face. It was a message I had never intended to transmit, a clumsy misdirection into the wrong inbox. My heart began hammering against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm that drowned out all other sound. I squeezed my eyes shut, pressing the phone against my chest as if I could physically stop the message from reaching its intended destination. When I opened them, the screen remained illuminated, mocking me with its unintended reach. A shaky hand reached out to snatch the device, my movements jerky and uncoordinated.

[story 3]

I reread the text, the words feeling heavy and wrong in my fingertips. Panic clawed at my throat, making it difficult to draw a steady breath. The recipient’s name was unfamiliar, a string of numbers that sent a jolt of pure alarm through me. I stood frozen by the window, the city lights blurring into streaks of frantic color. My palms were slick with sweat, and I could feel a distinct pressure building behind my eyes. Every instinct screamed at me to delete, to undo the action, but my fingers felt clumsy, slow, as if they were moving through thick mud. I kept glancing at the phone, waiting for the inevitable sound.
